event: janitor with occupational exposure to organic matter through gardening
admitted to the intensive care unit for ARDS
inhaled fluticasone propionate/salmeterol for the suspicion of early stage COPD
intermittent fever for 2 weeks before admission
Nonproductive cough started 7 days before admission
progressively increasing dyspnea started 7 days before admission
received 8 days of amoxicillin for the suspicion of bacterial pneumonia
received 5 days of pristinamycin for the suspicion of bacterial pneumonia
oxygen saturation 95% on high flow nasal oxygenation
White blood-cell count 10720 cells per mm3
lactate dehydrogenase blood level 882 U per liter
acute kidney failure without criteria for renal replacement therapy
Chest CT showed multiple bilateral ground-glass opacities with a crazy paving pattern
received a 7-day course of dexamethasone 20 mg daily for COVID-19
1 infusion of eculizumab 1200 mg on day 2 for COVID-19 clinical trial
antibiotics stopped upon negative tracheal aspirate on day 4
Culture isolated A. fumigatus with fungal load 20 CFU per 50 μL
Bacterial culture remained negative after 5 days of incubation
D-dimer level increased >20000 ng per milliliter
posaconazole resistant with MIC 0.5 μg per milliliter
itraconazole resistant with MIC >8 μg per milliliter
voriconazole resistant with MIC 2 μg per milliliter
isavuconazole resistant with MIC 4 μg per milliliter
bicarbonate ions level 17.2 mmol per liter
creatinine level 2.76 mg per deciliter (221 μmol per liter)
Specific A. fumigatus quantitative PCR positive with Cq value 26.3
serum β-D-Glucan negative at 10.4 pg per milliliter
serum β-D-Glucan negative at <7.8 pg per milliliter
BioFire FilmArray pneumonia plus panel negative
3 pulmonary nodules (7mm, 1mm, 1mm) in the right upper lobe
death attributed to presumed pulmonary embolism, not aspergillosis
classified as putative invasive aspergillosis according to Blot et al. criteria
transthoracic echocardiogram showed hypertrophy of the right ventricle
transthoracic echocardiogram showed dilation of the right ventricle
did not meet EORTC/MSGERC host criteria for invasive aspergillosis
no antifungal treatment administered due to rapid clinical evolution
